pattern: This is a mechanical three-stage pipeline -- reduce each position to a gcd against the running maximum seen so far, sort those reduced values, then greedily pair the smallest survivor with the largest survivor from both ends inward -- so correctness is just about executing the three stages in order without skipping the running-maximum update.
hints: Walk nums once, keeping a running maximum, and record gcd(nums[i], runningMax) at each step -- this running maximum must include nums[i] itself before taking the gcd.
Sort the resulting array; the pairing rule always combines the current smallest remaining value with the current largest remaining value.

Use two pointers closing in from both ends of the sorted array, summing gcd(low, high) each step and stopping once they meet or cross (an unpaired middle element on an odd-length array is simply skipped).

edge_inputs.description: statement-style example, three elements with the running max never changing
single element, no pairs possible at all
strictly decreasing values -- the running max locks onto the very first element
all identical values, every prefix gcd equals the value itself
coprime values throughout, most pair gcds collapse to 1
odd length -- the middle element after sorting must be left unpaired

stress.description: random values across the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: small value range so gcds are frequently non-trivial
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 30) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

oracle_python:
from math import gcd

class Solution:
    def gcdSum(self, nums):
        # independent construction: build prefixGcd via a list comprehension over running
        # maxima computed with itertools.accumulate, then pair by direct index arithmetic
        # instead of a two-pointer walk.
        from itertools import accumulate
        running_max = list(accumulate(nums, max))
        prefix_gcd = sorted(gcd(a, b) for a, b in zip(nums, running_max))
        n = len(prefix_gcd)
        total = 0
        for i in range(n // 2):
            total += gcd(prefix_gcd[i], prefix_gcd[n - 1 - i])
        return total

solutions.python:
from math import gcd

class Solution:
    def gcdSum(self, nums):
        mx = 0
        prefix_gcd = []
        for v in nums:
            mx = max(mx, v)
            prefix_gcd.append(gcd(v, mx))
        prefix_gcd.sort()
        total = 0
        i, j = 0, len(prefix_gcd) - 1
        while i < j:
            total += gcd(prefix_gcd[i], prefix_gcd[j])
            i += 1
            j -= 1
        return total
